governance-leadership.relevance: Defines ISMS scope and interested-party requirements to keep governance boundaries clear.
Requires leadership accountability, policy direction, and assigned responsibilities.
Supports top-level policy governance and communication.
Aligns security strategy, objectives, and oversight structure.

risk-management.relevance: Requires risk criteria and repeatable risk assessment methods.
Ensures treatment decisions and accepted risk are formally tracked.
Improves risk context and treatment priorities.
Requires managed lifecycle for identification, analysis, treatment, and review.

information-security-policy.relevance: Sets policy approval and communication expectations.
Provides policy structure and maintenance expectations.
Reinforces policy compliance and governance controls.

document-control.relevance: Defines document approval, revision control, and retention.
Links document quality to audit and review outcomes.
Requires measurable monitoring and corrective improvements.

asset-inventory.controlName: Inventory of information and other associated assets
asset-inventory.relevance: Requires complete asset records and ownership.
Requires asset lifecycle control and accountability.

information-classification.relevance: Requires information classification to drive handling requirements.
Aligns asset handling with classification and ownership.

awareness-training.relevance: Requires personnel awareness of security obligations.
Defines periodic awareness and training coverage.

hr-security.relevance: Supports pre-employment checks based on role risk.

hr-security.controlName: Responsibilities after termination or change of employment
hr-security.relevance: Requires controlled transition and offboarding responsibilities.

physical-security.relevance: Requires controlled entry to secure areas.

access-control.relevance: Requires controlled access principles and enforcement.
Requires granting, reviewing, and revoking access rights.
Covers identity lifecycle and access governance.

privileged-access.relevance: Requires special controls for privileged accounts.
Requires stronger oversight of administrative access.

authentication.relevance: Maps to identity verification and login controls.

logging-monitoring.relevance: Requires relevant log capture and protection.

vulnerability-management.relevance: Requires vulnerability identification and remediation governance.

change-management.relevance: Requires authorized, tested, and tracked changes.

configuration-management.relevance: Requires approved baselines and controlled deviations.

backup-recovery.relevance: Requires backup scope, retention, and restoration testing.

incident-management.controlName: Information security incident management planning and preparation

incident-management.relevance: Requires timely and structured incident response.

evidence-collection.relevance: Requires evidence integrity and chain-of-custody discipline.

supplier-security.relevance: Requires supplier security expectations and oversight.

supplier-security.controlName: Monitoring, review and change management of supplier services
supplier-security.relevance: Requires ongoing supplier monitoring and updates.
Covers due diligence and contractual safeguards.

cloud-security.controlName: Information security for use of cloud services
cloud-security.relevance: Defines secure cloud adoption and oversight requirements.

cloud-security.controlName: Information System Acquisition, Development, and Maintenance
cloud-security.relevance: Addresses secure system design and operation in cloud contexts.

network-security.relevance: Requires network protections aligned with business risk.
Covers protective and monitoring controls for network environments.

secure-development.relevance: Requires security integration across development phases.
Requires coding practices that reduce defects and exploitable weaknesses.

secure-development.controlName: Information System Acquisition, Development, and Maintenance

cryptography.relevance: Requires cryptography policy and key lifecycle governance.
Supports operational controls for cryptographic implementation.

privacy-pii.relevance: Requires controls for lawful and secure PII handling.
Connects PII handling to asset inventory and ownership.

business-continuity.relevance: Requires ICT preparedness for disruption scenarios.
Requires continuity plans and testing evidence.

performance-improvement.relevance: Requires metrics, audits, and management review.
Requires corrective actions and continual improvement.
Aligns KPIs, reviews, and improvement plans.
